sectionSubtitle: Traditional feasts and meals, made from scratch

items.name: Tagine Feast
items.subtitle: Ras el hanout clay pot tradition
dishes: Ras el hanout tagines (2 chickpea, 1 salmon, 1 chicken)
Sweet potatoes with smoked paprika yogurt/cashew sauce
Cinnamon spiced orange slices with pomegranate

items.name: Couscous Feast
items.subtitle: Build-your-own couscous bowls
dishes: Proteins: chicken · chickpeas (V) · lentil + sweet potato (V)
Toppings: cauliflower + carrot (V) · chermoula (V) · harissa (V) · yogurt · pickled radish + onion (V) · fresh herbs (V)
Dessert table: semolina cake · stuffed dates (V)
Drinks: pomegranate-mint-apple juice · ginger mint maple tea

items.name: Kofta Feast
items.subtitle: Vegan kofta with eight sides
dishes: Khizou mcharmel · Makouda · Roasted cauliflower with chermoula
Orange blossom ice cream · orange cream sorbet (V)

steps: Soak navy beans overnight, 8 hours minimum. Drain and rinse.
Build the base: heat olive oil, cook onions, celery, and garlic 12 minutes. Add tomato paste and stir until it darkens.
Bloom the spices: cumin, coriander, ginger, turmeric, paprika, cinnamon, black pepper, salt. Cook 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add beans and water, bring to a boil, simmer 45 minutes until beans begin to soften.
Add lentils and crushed tomatoes, simmer 30 more minutes until beans and lentils are completely tender.
Finish with cilantro, parsley, lemon juice, and olive oil. Refrigerate overnight.
Reheat gently, add water if too thick, re-season. Optional: poach salmon portions in simmering harira at service.
Char eggplants directly over flame or under a very hot broiler until skin is completely black and flesh has collapsed.
Peel, split open, and drain in a colander at least 20 minutes.
Cook garlic gently in olive oil, add cumin and paprika, then chopped tomatoes until jammy.
Add chopped eggplant, cook 15 minutes mashing until thick. Blend two-thirds smooth, fold back into the chunky third.
Off heat, stir in argan oil, preserved lemon, and lemon juice.
Fold in cilantro and parsley. Refrigerate overnight. Drizzle argan oil at service.
Combine garlic, sweet paprika, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, crushed fennel seeds, cayenne, cinnamon, harissa, olive oil, salt and pepper into a spice paste.
Mix paste into cold Beyond Meat ground. Don't overmix.
Fry one small test patty — taste and adjust seasoning before forming the whole batch.
Form into small sausage shapes or patties, keep uniform for even cooking. Refrigerate until service.
Cook at high heat with olive oil, turning occasionally until browned on all sides — about 8 minutes per batch. Don't crowd the pan.
Cut zucchini into thick half-rounds, about half an inch. Too thin and they go mushy.
Toss with harissa, olive oil, cumin, and salt. Let sit 10 minutes.
Spread in a single layer across sheet trays — do not overlap. Roast at 425°F for 22 minutes, turning once.
Squeeze lemon juice over and scatter torn fresh mint just before serving.
Shave fennel as thinly as possible (mandoline if you have one). Soak in cold water 10 minutes to crisp, drain well.
Peel oranges removing all pith, slice into rounds. Save any juice for the dressing.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, salt, pepper and reserved orange juice.
Toss fennel and orange with dressing just before serving. Add parsley and scatter fennel fronds. Serve within 30 minutes.
Rinse pearl barley in cold water until water runs clear.
Combine with water or light vegetable stock and salt. Bring to a boil, reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook 45 minutes until tender but still slightly chewy — should have some bite, not mushy.
Drain any excess liquid, stir in olive oil. Cover and keep warm until service.
Combine dried shiba wormwood, dried verbena louiza, and dried thyme in a teapot.
Pour boiling water over herbs. Steep 7 minutes — taste at 5 for gentle and floral, longer for more bitter and complex.
Strain into a serving vessel. Mix 1:1 with agave and add 2 tbs to freshly brewed mint tea.
Toast flour in a large dry heavy pan over medium heat, stirring constantly 20 minutes until golden brown and nutty. Do not stop stirring.
Toast almond flour over medium-low heat, stirring constantly 7 minutes until golden. It burns faster — watch carefully.
Toast sesame seeds in a dry pan, stirring 4 minutes until golden and fragrant. They will pop when ready.
Combine all toasted ingredients with cinnamon, ground anise, salt, and powdered sugar.
Warm honey so it flows. Pour argan oil and olive oil over the dry mixture. Mix with your hands — crumbly but holds its shape when pressed.
Taste and adjust. Pack into a sealed container. At service, mound in small bowls with a dusting of powdered sugar and cinnamon.
